response.justification: Sarah Smith demonstrates strong software development experience with a focus on full-stack and tooling, but her background lacks direct alignment with the embedded networking and security requirements of the role. While she has C/C++ experience and Linux familiarity, her recent work skews toward web and AI-agent infrastructure, with no explicit mention of security protocols, networking layers, or embedded firmware development.
strengths: Extensive software development experience (10+ years) with a track record of leadership in full-stack and tooling roles.
Proficiency in C/C++ (e.g., National Instruments role) and Linux, with exposure to low-latency systems and hardware-adjacent projects.
Strong problem-solving skills and contributions to open-source projects, demonstrating initiative and technical depth.
concerns: No explicit experience with security technologies (e.g., MACsec, 802.1X) or embedded networking protocols (e.g., NETCONF, SNMP).
Lacks native-level Japanese proficiency, a hard requirement for the role.
Recent work focuses heavily on web/AI-agent infrastructure, with limited evidence of embedded firmware or switch-chip development.
